variation_instruction.prompt: IMPORTANT: Implement from the inside out. Use the wishful programming approach. Write the internal code you wish you had, before you write the code that uses it.

llm_gate_results.reasoning: The implementation covers all explicit requirements: Dutch UI with 'Start' button on initial screen, 'Wat wil je oefenen?' question with 'Analoog'/'Digitaal'/'Beide' choices, game loop showing analog+digital clock pairs with one editable per round, randomized times via randomTime(), digital clock with individual digit up/down controls, analog clock with draggable hands, no client-side routing (single index.html), plain JS central store (createStore/createGameStore), placeholder tests via vitest that run with 'npm run test', reusable components/utilities, and responsive minimal CSS. The 'Beide' mode alternates which clock is editable, matching the intent of practicing both types. The store pattern uses subscriptions and mutation methods without any library.

Both test files contain meaningful, substantive assertions. dom-utils.test.js thoroughly tests qs, qsa, createElement, and emit utilities with real behavioral assertions (checking return values, element properties, event dispatch, bubbling). app-card.test.js tests custom element registration, shadow DOM, bidirectional attribute/property reflection, and event emission with concrete expect() calls. No tests are trivially passing, no assertions are missing, and no test cases appear to have been removed or weakened.

The implementation is visually sound. Layout uses clean CSS grid/flexbox with proper alignment. The analog clock SVG fits within its container, the digital clock spinner controls are well-structured, and all three screens (start, mode-select, game) have appropriate centering and spacing. The only notable finding is a missing btn--primary CSS modifier, but the base .btn class already provides identical primary styling. Responsive breakpoint at 540px correctly collapses the two-column clock grid to single column. Contrast on feedback messages (green/red) is adequate. No clipping, overflow, occlusion, or layout collapse issues found.

variation_instruction.prompt: IMPORTANT: Implement the outside first, the functional code, before the internal implementation.
